agents.name: analysis_agent
agents.system_prompt:
You are an Analysis Agent specialized in Pull Request analysis. ## Project Context (MASCA Analysis) ## Project Analysis ### README
# MLC LLM [![Installation](https://img.shields.io/badge/docs-latest-green)](https://llm.mlc.ai/docs/) [![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-apache_2-blue)](https://github.com/mlc-ai/mlc-llm/blob/main/LICENSE) [![Join Discoard](https://img.shields.io/badge/Join-Discord-7289DA?logo=discord&logoColor=white)](https://discord.gg/9Xpy2HGBuD) [![Related Repository: WebLLM](https://img.shields.io/badge/Related_Repo-WebLLM-fafbfc?logo=github)](https://github.com/mlc-ai/web-llm/) **Universal LLM Deployment Engine with ML Compilation** [Get Started](https://llm.mlc.ai/docs/get_started/quick_start) | [Documentation](https://llm.mlc.ai/docs) | [Blog](https://blog.mlc.ai/)
## About MLC LLM is a machine learning compiler and high-performance deployment engine for large language models. The mission of this project is to enable everyone to develop, optimize, and deploy AI models natively on everyone's platforms. 
AMD GPU NVIDIA GPU Apple GPU Intel GPU
Linux / Win ✅ Vulkan, ROCm ✅ Vulkan, CUDA N/A ✅ Vulkan
macOS ✅ Metal (dGPU) N/A ✅ Metal ✅ Metal (iGPU)
Web Browser ✅ WebGPU and WASM
iOS / iPadOS ✅ Metal on Apple A-series GPU
Android ✅ OpenCL on Adreno GPU ✅ OpenCL on Mali GPU
MLC LLM compiles and runs code on MLCEngine -- a unified high-performance LLM inference engine across the above platforms. MLCEngine provides OpenAI-compatible API available through REST server, python, javascript, iOS, Android, all backed by the same engine and compiler that we keep improving with the community. ## Get Started Please visit our [documentation](https://llm.mlc.ai/docs/) to get started with MLC LLM. - [Installation](https://llm.mlc.ai/docs/install/mlc_llm) - [Quick start](https://llm.mlc.ai/docs/get_started/quick_start) - [Introduction](https://llm.mlc.ai/docs/get_started/introduction) ## Citation Please consider citing our project if you find it useful: ```bibtex @software{mlc-llm, author = {{MLC team}}, title = {{MLC-LLM}}, url = {https://github.com/mlc-ai/mlc-llm}, year = {2023-2025} } ``` The underlying techniques of MLC LLM include:
References (Click to expand) ```bibtex @inproceedings{tensorir, author = {Feng, Siyuan and Hou, Bohan and Jin, Hongyi and Lin, Wuwei and Shao, Junru and Lai, Rui ## Your Task When you receive a PR title and body, you must: 1. **Analyze the request**: Understand what the PR wants to achieve 2. **Explore the code**: Use tools to navigate base_project and find relevant files/functions 3. **Identify targets**: Determine exactly which files and functions need to be modified 4. **Optimize MASCA**: Produce a version of the MASCA analysis focused on the specific PR ## Available Tools - `read_base_project_file(file_path)`: Read a file from the project - `list_base_project_directory(directory, pattern, recursive)`: List files/folders ## Required Output You must produce a structured output with: - `pr_title`: PR title - `pr_body`: PR body - `masca_optimized`: MASCA optimized for this specific PR - `files_to_modify`: List of files to modify with motivation - `functions_to_modify`: List of functions to modify with motivation - `analysis_summary`: Summary of your analysis ## Guidelines - Explore the code before drawing conclusions - Search for files with names relevant to the PR - Identify specific functions, not just generic files - Be precise with paths (relative to base_project) - For function names use the format "ClassName.method_name" for methods

output.masca_optimized:
Summary of intent
- The PR fixes how multiple per-token logprob JSON fragments (produced per-token by the sampler) are merged into the final OpenAI-style "logprobs" object for a single choice.
- The project currently produces per-token JSON fragments (SampleResult::GetLogProbJSON) and stores them in RequestStreamOutput::group_delta_logprob_json_strs.
- The JSONFFI layer (GetResponseFromStreamOutput) builds the streaming JSON response, but it didn't merge per-token logprob fragments into the OpenAI logprobs object correctly (or at all).

Where to change (high level)
1) Add a place to hold the merged logprobs in the OpenAI response data structure (both stream and final responses) so the merged result can be serialized.
2) Implement merging logic that:
   - Parses each per-token JSON fragment string (SampleResult::GetLogProbJSON output) into picojson::value
   - Extracts token text and logprob to populate arrays: tokens[], token_logprobs[]
   - Converts each per-token top_logprobs (an array of small objects produced by SampleResult) into a mapping object {token_string: logprob} and append it to top_logprobs[] (so result's top_logprobs is an array of mapping objects)
   - Sets text_offset (null) and other fields according to OpenAI spec
3) Call this merging logic when building streaming responses in JSONFFIEngine::GetResponseFromStreamOutput and set the merged logprobs on the ChatCompletionStreamResponseChoice before serializing.
4) Expose the logprobs field in ChatCompletionStreamResponseChoice and ChatCompletionResponseChoice AsJSON so it shows up in both streaming and final responses.

Concrete code locations (from code exploration)
- Per-token JSON is produced by: cpp/serve/data.cc::SampleResult::GetLogProbJSON (already implemented). It returns JSON strings per token which include fields: "token", "logprob", "bytes", "top_logprobs" (array of objects where each object is like {"token":..., "logprob":..., "bytes":[...]}).
- Those strings get put into RequestStreamOutput::group_delta_logprob_json_strs at: cpp/serve/request_state.cc::RequestStateEntryNode::GetDeltaRequestReturn (it pushes SampleResult::GetLogProbJSON into the vector used by streaming).
- The streaming JSON string is built in: cpp/json_ffi/json_ffi_engine.cc::JSONFFIEngineImpl::GetResponseFromStreamOutput
- The OpenAI response types and serialization are in: cpp/json_ffi/openai_api_protocol.h / openai_api_protocol.cc

Precise modifications (what and why)
- cpp/json_ffi/openai_api_protocol.h
  - Add an optional picojson::value (or picojson::object wrapper) member to ChatCompletionStreamResponseChoice and ChatCompletionResponseChoice to hold logprobs.
  - This allows AsJSON() to include the "logprobs" key when present.
- cpp/json_ffi/openai_api_protocol.cc
  - Update ChatCompletionStreamResponseChoice::AsJSON and ChatCompletionResponseChoice::AsJSON to add obj["logprobs"] when the new member is set.
- cpp/json_ffi/json_ffi_engine.cc
  - In JSONFFIEngineImpl::GetResponseFromStreamOutput, when iterating delta_outputs and choices, detect whether delta_output->group_delta_logprob_json_strs is present for the choice and if so:
    - Parse each per-token JSON string using picojson::parse
    - Build tokens array, token_logprobs array and top_logprobs array (transform per-token top_logprobs into map objects mapping token->logprob)
    - Construct a picojson::object for the merged logprobs: {"text_offset": null, "token_logprobs": [...], "tokens": [...], "top_logprobs": [ {token: logprob, ...}, ... ] }
    - Assign it to choice.logprobs (new member) so ChatCompletionStreamResponseChoice::AsJSON will include it.

Why these files/functions
- SampleResult::GetLogProbJSON (cpp/serve/data.cc) already creates single-token JSON fragments. The bug is not here (it produces per-token JSON correctly), but the merging of multiple such fragments into a single OpenAI-style logprobs object occurs upstream in the JSONFFI serialization layer.
- JSONFFIEngineImpl::GetResponseFromStreamOutput is the place that converts internal RequestStreamOutput into the final JSON sent to clients; that's the correct place to parse/merge group_delta_logprob_json_strs into the choices' "logprobs" field.
- openai_api_protocol.{h,cc} define the response types and their JSON serialization; these must be extended to carry and serialize the merged logprobs field.

Edge cases and robustness
- Per-token JSON strings must be parsed robustly; on parse error we should skip/ignore and not crash (but report or leave out logprobs for that choice).
- If per-token top_logprobs are empty, top_logprobs for that token should be an empty object in the final array.
- Keep text_offset null (as current code does in examples) unless a proper offset is available.

Testing
- Add unit / integration tests that simulate RequestStreamOutput with multiple per-token logprob JSON strings and assert that final JSON contains correct tokens array, token_logprobs array and top_logprobs array of mapping objects in the same order as tokens.
- Validate both streaming and final (non-stream) responses include merged logprobs when generation_cfg->logprobs is set.

Summary: Implement merging inside JSONFFI (GetResponseFromStreamOutput), and expose logprobs in openai_api_protocol types so they are serialized into the final OpenAI-compatible response. This matches the difference between the earlier incorrect response and the new correct response shown in the PR description.

files_to_modify.reason: Add storage for merged logprobs in response choice types (streaming and final) so merged logprobs can be serialized into the final OpenAI-compatible JSON.
Update ChatCompletionStreamResponseChoice::AsJSON and ChatCompletionResponseChoice::AsJSON to include the optional merged logprobs value when present.

Implement the merging logic that parses per-token logprob JSON fragments from RequestStreamOutput->group_delta_logprob_json_strs and builds a single OpenAI-style "logprobs" object for each choice; attach it to the choice before serializing.

functions_to_modify.reason: Include the merged logprobs object (new member) into the serialized JSON for streaming response choices.
Include the merged logprobs object (new member) into the serialized JSON for final non-streaming response choices.

Parse per-token logprob JSON strings from delta_output->group_delta_logprob_json_strs, merge them into a single OpenAI-style "logprobs" object (tokens[], token_logprobs[], top_logprobs[] of mapping objects, text_offset null), and set this object on each choice before serializing the response.

output.analysis_summary: I scanned the C++ server-side code that generates and serializes OpenAI-compatible JSON responses. Per-token logprob fragments are produced by SampleResult::GetLogProbJSON (cpp/serve/data.cc) and are propagated into RequestStreamOutput::group_delta_logprob_json_strs inside request_state. The JSON serialization/back-end that forms the final stream is JSONFFIEngineImpl::GetResponseFromStreamOutput (cpp/json_ffi/json_ffi_engine.cc) and the OpenAI response types/serializers live in cpp/json_ffi/openai_api_protocol.{h,cc}. The project currently does not merge the per-token logprob fragments into the single OpenAI-style "logprobs" object correctly; the fix should be to (1) add an optional logprobs field to the choice data types, (2) implement merging/parsing/transformation of per-token JSON fragments into a combined object in GetResponseFromStreamOutput, and (3) serialize it via the modified AsJSON methods. The precise changes are to three files: openai_api_protocol.h, openai_api_protocol.cc, and json_ffi_engine.cc, and to the three functions listed above.

agents.name: context_planner_agent
agents.system_prompt:
You are a Context Planner Agent specialized in step plan generation.

## Your Task
You receive from the Analysis Agent:
- PR title and body
- Optimized MASCA
- List of files to modify
- List of functions to modify

You must:
1. **Retrieve context**: Use tools to read the context files of the identified functions
2. **Analyze dependencies**: Understand how the functions interact
3. **Generate step plan**: Create a detailed step-by-step plan

## Available Tools
- `read_context_file(file_path)`: Read a pre-generated context file
- `list_context_files(directory, pattern)`: List available context files
- `read_call_graph(section)`: Read information from the call graph

## Required Output
You must produce a `step_plan` with:
- `summary`: 1-2 sentence summary of what the PR does
- `steps`: Ordered list of steps, each with:
  - `operation`: What to do (e.g., "Add validation method")
  - `file_to_modify`: Relative path of the file to modify (e.g., "src/module.py")
  - `function_to_modify`: Name of the function to modify (e.g., "ClassName.method_name")
  - `reason`: Technical motivation
  - `side_effects`: Potential impacts

## IMPORTANT RULE for function_to_modify
- If the file to modify is a Python file (.py), specify the function/method to modify
- If the file is NOT a Python file (e.g., .js, .html, .css, .json, .yaml, .md, etc.),
  the value of `function_to_modify` MUST be null

## Guidelines for Steps
- Each step must be atomic and implementable
- The order of steps must be logical (dependencies first)
- For Python files: always specify the function in the format "FunctionName" or "ClassName.method_name"
- For non-Python files: function_to_modify must be null
- The side_effects should mention tests, dependents, and API impacts
- You MUST generate a number of step EQUAL to the number of the python functions you are intendeed to modify nomore neither less

## Step Examples

### For Python files:
```json
{
  "operation": "Add time_spanned_alpha method",
  "file_to_modify": "manimlib/animation/animation.py",
  "function_to_modify": "Animation.time_spanned_alpha",
  "reason": "To compute animation progress based on actual elapsed time",
  "side_effects": "May require downstream animations to call this method"
}
```

### For non-Python files:
```json
{
  "operation": "Update API endpoint configuration",
  "file_to_modify": "config/routes.yaml",
  "function_to_modify": null,
  "reason": "Add new route for the authentication endpoint",
  "side_effects": "Requires server restart to apply changes"
}
```
agents.input_prompt:
Generate an implementation plan for this PR:

## PR Title
Fixed log prob result generation

## PR Body
Fixes the merging logic of logprobs of different tokens  for the same choice.


Earlier response 

```{
    "id": "cmpl-0e79013e278c497a8926be59641d0381",
    "choices": [
        {
            "finish_reason": "length",
            "index": 0,
            "logprobs": {
                "text_offset": null,
                "token_logprobs": [
                    0.0
                ],
                "tokens": [
                    ""
                ],
                "top_logprobs": [
                    {
                        "": -23.0259,
                        "<unk>": -23.0259,
                        "<s>": -23.0259,
                        "</s>": -23.0259
                    }
                ]
            },
            "text": "2, and "
        }
    ],
    "created": 1741063066,
    "model": "/home/annanyac/mlc-llm/dist/Llama-2-7b-chat-hf-q0f16-MLC/",
    "object": "text_completion",
    "usage": {
        "prompt_tokens": 6,
        "completion_tokens": 5,
        "total_tokens": 11,
        "extra": null
    }
}
```


New response 

```{
    "id": "cmpl-d6b2bc3820f8441fbfbe9d5d0be3d9ce",
    "choices": [
        {
            "finish_reason": "length",
            "index": 0,
            "logprobs": {
                "text_offset": null,
                "token_logprobs": [
                    0.0,
                    -0.912338,
                    -0.47704,
                    -0.111992
                ],
                "tokens": [
                    "2",
                    ".",
                    "",
                    ""
                ],
                "top_logprobs": [
                    {
                        "2": 0.0,
                        "<unk>": -23.0259,
                        "<s>": -23.0259,
                        "</s>": -23.0259,
                        "": -23.0259
                    },
                    {
                        ".": -0.912338,
                        ",": -1.07249,
                        "": -1.36156,
                        "<unk>": -23.0259,
                        "<s>": -23.0259
                    },
                    {
                        "": -1.39891,
                        "So": -2.54735,
                        "In": -2.91454,
                        "<unk>": -23.0259
                    },
                    {
                        "": -0.111992,
                        "So": -2.2448,
                        "<unk>": -23.0259,
                        "<s>": -23.0259,
                        "</s>": -23.0259
                    }
                ]
            },
            "text": "2.\n\n"
        }
    ],
    "created": 1741064363,
    "model": "/mnt/data/ehdd1/home/models/mlc/Llama-2-7b-chat-hf-q0f16-MLC/",
    "object": "text_completion",
    "usage": {
        "prompt_tokens": 6,
        "completion_tokens": 5,
        "total_tokens": 11,
        "extra": null
    }
}```

Explore the source code to identify the files and functions to modify.

## MASCA Analysis (optimized)
Summary of intent
- The PR fixes how multiple per-token logprob JSON fragments (produced per-token by the sampler) are merged into the final OpenAI-style "logprobs" object for a single choice.
- The project currently produces per-token JSON fragments (SampleResult::GetLogProbJSON) and stores them in RequestStreamOutput::group_delta_logprob_json_strs.

   - Parses each per-token JSON fragment string (SampleResult::GetLogProbJSON output) into picojson::value
   - Extracts token text and logprob to populate arrays: tokens[], token_logprobs[]
   - Converts each per-token top_logprobs (an array of small objects produced by SampleResult) into a mapping object {token_string: logprob} and append it to top_logprobs[] (so result's top_logprobs is an array of mapping objects)
   - Sets text_offset (null) and other fields according to OpenAI spec
3) Call this merging logic when building streaming responses in JSONFFIEngine::GetResponseFromStreamOutput and set the merged logprobs on the ChatCompletionStreamResponseChoice before serializing.
4) Expose the logprobs field in ChatCompletionStreamResponseChoice and ChatCompletionResponseChoice AsJSON so it shows up in both streaming and final responses.

- Those strings get put into RequestStreamOutput::group_delta_logprob_json_strs at: cpp/serve/request_state.cc::RequestStateEntryNode::GetDeltaRequestReturn (it pushes SampleResult::GetLogProbJSON into the vector used by streaming).
- The streaming JSON string is built in: cpp/json_ffi/json_ffi_engine.cc::JSONFFIEngineImpl::GetResponseFromStreamOutput
- The OpenAI response types and serialization are in: cpp/json_ffi/openai_api_protocol.h / openai_api_protocol.cc

Precise modifications (what and why)
- cpp/json_ffi/openai_api_protocol.h
  - Add an optional picojson::value (or picojson::object wrapper) member to ChatCompletionStreamResponseChoice and ChatCompletionResponseChoice to hold logprobs.
  - This allows AsJSON() to include the "logprobs" key when present.
- cpp/json_ffi/openai_api_protocol.cc

- cpp/json_ffi/json_ffi_engine.cc
  - In JSONFFIEngineImpl::GetResponseFromStreamOutput, when iterating delta_outputs and choices, detect whether delta_output->group_delta_logprob_json_strs is present for the choice and if so:
    - Parse each per-token JSON string using picojson::parse
    - Build tokens array, token_logprobs array and top_logprobs array (transform per-token top_logprobs into map objects mapping token->logprob)
    - Construct a picojson::object for the merged logprobs: {"text_offset": null, "token_logprobs": [...], "tokens": [...], "top_logprobs": [ {token: logprob, ...}, ... ] }
    - Assign it to choice.logprobs (new member) so ChatCompletionStreamResponseChoice::AsJSON will include it.

Why these files/functions
- SampleResult::GetLogProbJSON (cpp/serve/data.cc) already creates single-token JSON fragments. The bug is not here (it produces per-token JSON correctly), but the merging of multiple such fragments into a single OpenAI-style logprobs object occurs upstream in the JSONFFI serialization layer.
- JSONFFIEngineImpl::GetResponseFromStreamOutput is the place that converts internal RequestStreamOutput into the final JSON sent to clients; that's the correct place to parse/merge group_delta_logprob_json_strs into the choices' "logprobs" field.
- openai_api_protocol.{h,cc} define the response types and their JSON serialization; these must be extended to carry and serialize the merged logprobs field.

Edge cases and robustness
- Per-token JSON strings must be parsed robustly; on parse error we should skip/ignore and not crash (but report or leave out logprobs for that choice).
- If per-token top_logprobs are empty, top_logprobs for that token should be an empty object in the final array.
- Keep text_offset null (as current code does in examples) unless a proper offset is available.

Testing
- Add unit / integration tests that simulate RequestStreamOutput with multiple per-token logprob JSON strings and assert that final JSON contains correct tokens array, token_logprobs array and top_logprobs array of mapping objects in the same order as tokens.
- Validate both streaming and final (non-stream) responses include merged logprobs when generation_cfg->logprobs is set.

Summary: Implement merging inside JSONFFI (GetResponseFromStreamOutput), and expose logprobs in openai_api_protocol types so they are serialized into the final OpenAI-compatible response. This matches the difference between the earlier incorrect response and the new correct response shown in the PR description.

## Files to Modify
- cpp/json_ffi/openai_api_protocol.h: Add storage for merged logprobs in response choice types (streaming and final) so merged logprobs can be serialized into the final OpenAI-compatible JSON.
- cpp/json_ffi/openai_api_protocol.cc: Update ChatCompletionStreamResponseChoice::AsJSON and ChatCompletionResponseChoice::AsJSON to include the optional merged logprobs value when present.
- cpp/json_ffi/json_ffi_engine.cc: Implement the merging logic that parses per-token logprob JSON fragments from RequestStreamOutput->group_delta_logprob_json_strs and builds a single OpenAI-style "logprobs" object for each choice; attach it to the choice before serializing.

## Functions to Modify
- ChatCompletionStreamResponseChoice::AsJSON (cpp/json_ffi/openai_api_protocol.cc): Include the merged logprobs object (new member) into the serialized JSON for streaming response choices.
- ChatCompletionResponseChoice::AsJSON (cpp/json_ffi/openai_api_protocol.cc): Include the merged logprobs object (new member) into the serialized JSON for final non-streaming response choices.
- JSONFFIEngineImpl::GetResponseFromStreamOutput (cpp/json_ffi/json_ffi_engine.cc): Parse per-token logprob JSON strings from delta_output->group_delta_logprob_json_strs, merge them into a single OpenAI-style "logprobs" object (tokens[], token_logprobs[], top_logprobs[] of mapping objects, text_offset null), and set this object on each choice before serializing the response.

## Analysis Summary
I scanned the C++ server-side code that generates and serializes OpenAI-compatible JSON responses. Per-token logprob fragments are produced by SampleResult::GetLogProbJSON (cpp/serve/data.cc) and are propagated into RequestStreamOutput::group_delta_logprob_json_strs inside request_state. The JSON serialization/back-end that forms the final stream is JSONFFIEngineImpl::GetResponseFromStreamOutput (cpp/json_ffi/json_ffi_engine.cc) and the OpenAI response types/serializers live in cpp/json_ffi/openai_api_protocol.{h,cc}. The project currently does not merge the per-token logprob fragments into the single OpenAI-style "logprobs" object correctly; the fix should be to (1) add an optional logprobs field to the choice data types, (2) implement merging/parsing/transformation of per-token JSON fragments into a combined object in GetResponseFromStreamOutput, and (3) serialize it via the modified AsJSON methods. The precise changes are to three files: openai_api_protocol.h, openai_api_protocol.cc, and json_ffi_engine.cc, and to the three functions listed above.

steps.operation: Add storage for merged logprobs to OpenAI response choice types
steps.reason: We need a place on the response choice objects (both streaming and final) to hold the merged OpenAI-style "logprobs" object so it can be serialized. Adding an optional picojson::value (or picojson::object wrapper) member to ChatCompletionStreamResponseChoice and ChatCompletionResponseChoice enables storing the merged result produced later in the pipeline.
steps.side_effects: Changes struct/class layout in header — requires full rebuild. Downstream code that constructs these choices may need to be updated to default-initialize or clear the new member. No API contract break for callers that ignore unknown fields, but serialized JSON may include new keys once populated; update tests and any code that parses exactly-shaped JSON.

steps.operation: Serialize logprobs in choice AsJSON methods
steps.reason: ChatCompletionStreamResponseChoice::AsJSON and ChatCompletionResponseChoice::AsJSON must include the new "logprobs" field in the output JSON when the merged member is set. Without this change the merged logprobs stored on the choice will never appear in the serialized OpenAI-compatible JSON.
steps.side_effects: Alters output JSON shape when logprobs are present — consumers should accept the new field. Tests that assert exact JSON strings must be updated. If the logprobs member contains malformed picojson::value, ensure serialization handles it gracefully (the implementation should check presence/validity before inserting).

steps.operation: Implement merging/parsing of per-token logprob JSON fragments and attach merged object to choices
steps.reason: JSONFFIEngineImpl::GetResponseFromStreamOutput receives RequestStreamOutput which carries per-token logprob JSON fragments in group_delta_logprob_json_strs. This function must parse each fragment (picojson::parse), extract token text and logprob, transform per-token top_logprobs into an array of mapping objects {token: logprob}, build the OpenAI-style merged object {"text_offset": null, "token_logprobs": [...], "tokens": [...], "top_logprobs": [...]} and set it on the corresponding ChatCompletionStreamResponseChoice (and/or ChatCompletionResponseChoice) member so serialization emits it.
steps.side_effects: Introduces CPU and memory overhead due to JSON parsing and object construction during streaming; ensure efficient parsing and avoid throwing errors to callers. Must handle parse failures robustly by skipping invalid fragments and not crashing the stream. Affects streaming and final responses: tests and any downstream components consuming logprobs need to be updated/verified. May require synchronization if multiple threads touch the same RequestStreamOutput structure (verify existing concurrency guarantees).

step_plan.summary: Add a merged OpenAI-style "logprobs" field to response choice types and implement merging logic in the JSONFFI layer to parse per-token logprob fragments and produce a single tokens/token_logprobs/top_logprobs object per choice so it is serialized in streaming and final responses.
